agent2_snippets.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2339
agent2_snippets.content: Obstetrics excision is a broad surgical category in obstetric care that encompasses several specific procedures. It includes the surgical treatment of miscarriage of any trimester, as well as the surgical treatment of missed abortion of the second trimester, missed miscarriage of the first trimester, and missed miscarriage of the third trimester. In addition, Obstetrics excision also comprises termination of an ectopic pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم) and lower‑segment cesarean delivery (ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي). Together, these child procedures illustrate the range of obstetric conditions that can be managed through excisional surgical techniques.
